race.comment: Competitive. SENJUTSU (14) raced on the pace first-up beaten 1.4 lengths over 1300m at Hawkesbury. Had trialled well and has more to give. Has the tongue tie on for the first time and using the inside barrier will be hard to beat. CUBAN CIGAR (12) deserved his win here this grade over 1400m and he's had to endure wide runs since in stronger races. He's going to need some luck from the awkward barrier and will go close if he gets it. CHOKUTO (3) and MONOPOLISTIC (1) both resume for the Waller yard and have to be kept safe. The former led and just missed over a mile at Caulfield Heath when last seen and is capable of pushing forward. Has trialled well and has upside. The latter brings a deserved Canterbury win over 1550m and goes fresh. Has the blinkers on for the first time and a small claim, but the gate is sticky. SAVAII (2) may need one more, but maps well and has the ability. The late betting will guide.
